title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: clerk keeps tabs — clerkR keeps publication-ready.

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: clerkR transforms standard R data frames publication-ready tables biomedical neuroscience manuscripts. Rather wrestling formatting time, clerkR reduces common table types handful opinionated constructor functions share consistent theming, domain grouping, footnote handling, unified rendering pipeline Word/PDF (gt) interactive HTML (reactable) output.

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
text: 📋 Five table archetypes covering ~90% appears biomed paper 🗂️ Domain/section grouping — organise rows labelled headers 📝 Automatic footnotes log-transformed variables FDR correction 🎨 Dual render targets — render_gt() Word/PDF, render_reactable() HTML 🔢 Significance annotation — p-values, FDR-corrected p, stars (toggleable) 🧩 Composable — constructors return clerk_tbl S3 object

title: clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
code: clerkR/ ├── R/ │   ├── clerkR-package.R       # Package-level documentation │   ├── tbl_descriptive.R      # Archetype 1: Table 1 / descriptive │   ├── render.R               # render_gt() and render_reactable() │   └── clerk_example.R        # Synthetic example dataset docs ├── data/ │   └── clerk_example.rda      # Synthetic example dataset ├── data-raw/ │   └── clerk_example.R        # Script to generate example data ├── tests/testthat/ │   └── test-tbl_descriptive.R ├── man/                       # Auto-generated by roxygen2 ├── DESCRIPTION ├── NAMESPACE └── README.md

clerkR — Publication-ready tables for R
code: library(clerkR)  # Descriptive / Table 1 tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group   = sex,   domains = list(     "Metabolic"     = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Anthropometric"= c("waist", "systolic_bp"),     "Cognitive"     = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),     "Mental health" = c("bdi", "panas_neg", "life_satisfaction")   ),   log_vars = c("tmt_time"),   fdr      = TRUE ) |>   render_gt(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex")

what: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
title: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
text: clerkR transforms standard R data frames publication-ready tables across handful common archetypes found biomedical neuroscience manuscripts.

title: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
text: Descriptive / Table 1 (tbl_descriptive()): sample characteristics group comparisons, mean ± SD, t-test / chi-squared statistics. Heritability (tbl_heritability()): h2, confidence intervals, LRT p, variance components. Correlation (tbl_correlation()): r, p, FDR-corrected p, domain grouping. Regression (tbl_regression()): beta, SE, CI, p, FDR annotation. Simple descriptive (tbl_simple()): means/SDs, inferential test.

title: clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research — clerkR-package
text: constructors return clerk_tbl S3 object. Use render_gt() Word/PDF output render_reactable() interactive HTML.

what: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
text: Returns vector n hex colours interpolated along clerkR diverging scale: dusty terracotta (#D4907E) — neutral -white (#F0EEEC) — navy (#293681). Suitable use gt::data_color() scales::col_numeric().

title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
text: n Integer. Number colour steps (default 9). Use odd number include neutral midpoint. reverse Logical. Reverse scale direction (default FALSE, terracotta = low, navy = high).

title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging
text: character vector n hex colour codes.

title: Build a clerkR diverging colour scale — clerk_diverging

what: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
title: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
text: synthetic dataset 300 participants demographic, metabolic, anthropometric, cognitive, mental health variables, designed illustrate tbl_descriptive() clerkR table constructors. values simulated bear relation real study.

title: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
text: data frame 300 rows 12 variables: sex Factor: "Female" / "Male". age Age years (numeric). hdl HDL cholesterol mmol/L (numeric). glucose Fasting glucose mmol/L (numeric). bmi Body mass index kg/m² (numeric). waist Waist circumference cm (numeric). systolic_bp Systolic blood pressure mmHg (numeric). tmt_time Trail Making Test completion time seconds, log-scale analysis recommended (numeric). verbal_fluency Verbal fluency score — number words 60 s (numeric). bdi Beck Depression Inventory total score (numeric). panas_neg PANAS negative affect subscale score (numeric). life_satisfaction Life Satisfaction Scale total score (numeric).

title: Synthetic example dataset for clerkR — clerk_example
text: Simulated data generated data-raw/clerk_example.R.

title: clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette
text: Returns named clerkR colour palette character vector hex codes. Individual colours can accessed name via clerk_colour(). palette pairs formal cool-blue range (navy → light teal → near-white) dusty terracotta warm pole diverging scales.

title: clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette
clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette
text: named character vector hex codes.

title: clerkR colour palette — clerk_palette

what: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
title: Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
text: Applies clerkR visual style existing gt_tbl object: light teal column headers navy text, near-white row group bars, clean borders, consistent typography. Can applied gt pipeline. function called automatically render_gt() render_latex(); need directly building gt table outside tbl_* constructors.

Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table — clerk_theme
text: gt_tbl gt_tbl object.

text: gt_tbl object clerkR styling applied.

what: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — render
title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — render
text: Dispatches correct renderer based output slot set construction time (tbl_descriptive(..., output = "gt"|"html"|"latex")). render arguments (title, subtitle, footnote) can supplied forwarded underlying renderer.

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — render
code: render(x, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, footnote = NULL, ...)

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — render
text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional character string table title. subtitle Optional character string table subtitle. footnote Optional additional footnote text. ... Passed underlying render_gt(), render_reactable(), render_latex().

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — render
text: gt_tbl, reactable, knit_asis object depending output slot x.

title: Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format — render

what: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
text: Renders clerk_tbl gt table clerkR styling applied via clerk_theme(). Domain groupings become row-group labels; log-transformed variables receive automatic footnote. Typically called indirectly via render().

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
code: render_gt(x, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, footnote = NULL, ...)

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional table title. subtitle Optional table subtitle. footnote Optional additional footnote. ... Reserved future use.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt
text: gt_tbl object.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF) — render_gt

what: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
text: Renders clerk_tbl LaTeX table via gt::as_latex(). result can included directly .Rnw .Rmd/Quarto document compiled LaTeX engine. Typically called indirectly via render().

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
code: render_latex(x, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, footnote = NULL, ...)

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional table title (used \caption{}). subtitle Optional subtitle appended caption. footnote Optional additional footnote. ... Reserved future use.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
text: knit_asis character object containing LaTeX table source.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table — render_latex
code:

text: x clerk_tbl object. title Optional character string displayed table. ... Passed reactable::reactable().

text: reactable htmlwidget.

title: Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table — render_reactable

what: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
text: Produces descriptive/Table 1-style summary data frame, optional group comparisons. Continuous variables summarised mean ± SD compared independent-samples t-test (two groups) one-way ANOVA (three groups). Categorical variables summarised n (%) compared chi-squared test.

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
code: tbl_descriptive(   data,   group = NULL,   vars = NULL,   domains = list(),   log_vars = character(0),   digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   fdr = FALSE,   overall = TRUE,   output = c("gt", "html", "latex") )

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
text: data data frame. group <tidy-select> Unquoted name grouping variable. NULL (default) overall sample summarised without comparison column. vars <tidy-select> Variables include. Defaults columns except group. domains named list mapping variable names domain/section labels, e.g. list("Metabolic" = c("hdl", "glucose"), "Anthropometric" = c("bmi", "waist")). Variables mentioned placed unlabelled section. log_vars Character vector variable names log-transformed prior analysis. footnote appended noting values shown raw scale. digits Integer. Number decimal places continuous variables (default 2). p_digits Integer. Number decimal places p-values (default 3). fdr Logical. Apply BH FDR correction p-values across tests (default FALSE). overall Logical. Include overall (ungrouped) column alongside group columns (default TRUE). output Character string specifying render target. One "gt" (default, Word/PDF via gt), "html" (interactive reactable), "latex" (LaTeX via gt::as_latex()). value stored returned object used render() dispatch correct renderer automatically.

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
text: clerk_tbl object (list class "clerk_tbl") containing: table data frame one row per variable. domains domain list supplied user. log_vars log-transformed variable names. type Character string "descriptive". group Name grouping variable, NULL. output render target: "gt", "html", "latex".

title: Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1) — tbl_descriptive
code: # gt output (default) — pipe into render() tbl_descriptive(   clerk_example,   group    = sex,   domains  = list(     "Metabolic"    = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),     "Cognitive"    = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),     "Mental health"= c("bdi", "panas_neg")   ),   log_vars = "tmt_time",   output   = "gt" ) |> render(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex") #> Warning: invalid factor level, NA generated     Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex            n       Overall       Female       Male       Statistic       p     NA     age 300 46.76 ± 14.15 46.42 ± 13.63 47.35 ± 15.09 t = -0.53 5.992006e-01waist 300 92.07 ± 11.56 92.14 ± 11.32 91.93 ± 12.03 t = 0.15 8.792922e-01systolic_bp 300 123.25 ± 17.77 120.36 ± 17.86 128.48 ± 16.45 t = -3.97 9.521407e-05life_satisfaction 300 18.02 ± 4.23 17.40 ± 4.25 19.14 ± 3.99 t = -3.54 4.880922e-04Metabolic     hdl 300 46.81 ± 10.11 48.32 ± 9.78 44.08 ± 10.18 t = 3.50 5.599878e-04glucose 300 91.68 ± 24.65 92.61 ± 24.49 90.01 ± 24.96 t = 0.87 3.863309e-01bmi 300 26.15 ± 4.91 26.43 ± 4.84 25.64 ± 5.01 t = 1.32 1.895409e-01Cognitive     tmt_time1 300 129.24 ± 64.98 132.81 ± 66.77 122.81 ± 61.40 t = 1.31 1.916489e-01verbal_fluency 300 14.98 ± 4.63 15.08 ± 4.44 14.79 ± 4.97 t = 0.52 6.062013e-01Mental health     bdi 300 13.64 ± 9.26 15.07 ± 8.97 11.05 ± 9.26 t = 3.65 3.325514e-04panas_neg 300 20.98 ± 7.48 22.50 ± 7.27 18.25 ± 7.09 t = 4.92 1.662527e-061 Log-transformed variables shown on raw scale.
